judgment.summary: Snowflake shows strong revenue growth and healthy gross margins, but is unprofitable on an operating and net basis with high leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter (Jan 2026) EPS of $0.32 beat estimates of $0.27 by 17.8%. Next earnings date is May 27, 2026.
positives: Revenue grew 30.1% year-over-year, indicating strong top-line demand.
Gross margin of 67.2% is robust, suggesting efficient cost of goods sold.
Free cash flow of $1.59B (TTM) demonstrates significant cash generation from operations.
Analyst growth estimates for the current fiscal year are a strong 43.5%.
The mean analyst target of $229.24 suggests significant upside potential from the current price of $169.55.
negatives: Forward P/E of 69.95x is very high, implying aggressive growth expectations are priced in.
Operating margin of -33.2% and profit margin of -28.4% show the company is not yet profitable.
Return on equity of -53.9% and return on assets of -9.9% indicate poor profitability relative to equity and assets.
Debt/Equity ratio of 142.46% is high, suggesting significant financial leverage.
PEG ratio of 4.60x is elevated, indicating that expected growth may not fully justify the current valuation.

news.paragraph: Snowflake (SNOW) is trading around $169.55, with recent news indicating a positive outlook from analysts. Bank of America has notably raised its price target for the stock, first to $195 and then to $205, suggesting confidence in the company's upcoming performance. Analysts at Bank of America anticipate Snowflake will deliver a strong quarter, and the company is also being compared favorably against DELL in the AI infrastructure space.
